name: Circular through the hills of Castillo and Padrastro from Atienza
name_original: Circular por los cerros del Castillo y del Padrastro desde Atienza
region_intro: Jogging routes around Atienza traverse a landscape characterized by mountains, a plateau setting, and picturesque scenery. The region features varied terrain, including cereal fields, pastures, and the Sierra de Pela. Runners can explore routes with ascents, descents, and flatter paths through natural environments. The historic town also offers urban jogging experiences through its medieval layout of cobbled streets and ancient walls. Best jogging routes around Atienza The most popular jogging route is Circular through the hills of Castillo and Padrastro from Atienza, a 4.6 miles (7.4 km) trail that takes 55 minutes to complete. This route offers views of the surrounding hills and the historic castle. Another top favourite among local runners is GR 167: Prádena de Atienza to Cueva del Oso Trail, a moderate 5.9 miles (9.6 km) path. This trail leads through natural landscapes with varied elevation. Local runners also love the Prádena de Atienza – Sierra de Alto Rey loop from Prádena de Atienza, a 14.8 miles (23.9 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain, often completed in about 3 hours 36 minutes. Jogging around Atienza is defined by its mountainous terrain, cereal fields, and historic town features, including the imposing Atienza Castle. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging routes. The routes in Atienza are highly rated by the komoot community. More than 60 runners have used komoot to explore Atienza's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Atienza?
A: There are several running routes around Atienza, with komoot listing 7 dedicated jogging tours. These range from moderate to difficult, offering varied terrain for different fitness levels.

Q: Are there easy running routes for beginners in Atienza?
A: While there are no routes specifically classified as 'easy', Atienza offers 4 moderate running routes that could be suitable for beginners looking for a slight challenge. These paths provide a good introduction to the region's diverse landscapes.

Q: Where can I find longer running trails for endurance training near Atienza?
A: For longer runs, consider the challenging &lt;strong&gt;&lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20142648" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Prádena de Atienza – Sierra de Alto Rey loop from Prádena de Atienza&lt;/a&gt;&lt;/strong&gt;, which spans 23.9 km (14.8 miles) and takes approximately 3 hours 36 minutes to complete. Another option is the &lt;strong&gt;&lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20142637" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto Rey Peak (1,858 m) – Prádena de Atienza loop from Prádena de Atienza&lt;/a&gt;&lt;/strong&gt;, a 23.6 km (14.7 miles) route with significant elevation.

Q: Are there any circular running routes around Atienza?
A: Yes, Atienza offers several circular routes. A popular option is the &lt;strong&gt;&lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1032359021/circular-through-the-hills-of-castillo-and-padrastro-from-atienza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular through the hills of Castillo and Padrastro from Atienza&lt;/a&gt;&lt;/strong&gt;, a 7.4 km (4.6 miles) trail that provides views of the historic castle and surrounding hills. Another is the &lt;strong&gt;&lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20142600" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiendelaencina Silver Mines – Hiendelaencina loop from Hiendelaencina&lt;/a&gt;&lt;/strong&gt;, a 10.5 km (6.5 miles) moderate route.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on running routes in Atienza?
A: Atienza's running routes feature a varied landscape, including mountainous terrain, a plateau setting, cereal fields, and pastures. You'll encounter a mix of ascents, descents, and flatter paths. The historic town itself also offers urban jogging experiences through its medieval cobbled streets.

Q: Are there scenic viewpoints or historical landmarks along the running trails?
A: Absolutely! Many routes offer stunning views. The imposing &lt;strong&gt;&lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2054767" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Atienza&lt;/a&gt;&lt;/strong&gt;, perched on a rocky hill, provides spectacular panoramic views. You can also explore the medieval town layout, declared a Historic-Artistic Site. The &lt;strong&gt;&lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4436053" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Atienza Castle&lt;/a&gt;&lt;/strong&gt; highlight is a great spot for photos.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Atienza?
A: The komoot community highly rates the running experience in Atienza. Runners often praise the varied terrain, the picturesque scenery, and the unique combination of natural beauty with historical elements like the castle and medieval town. The network offers options for different ability levels, making it accessible to many.

Q: Is there a good place to find fresh water during a run?
A: Yes, the &lt;strong&gt;&lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2615897" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Miedes de Atienza Spring&lt;/a&gt;&lt;/strong&gt; is a natural spring located amidst pastures and hills. It can serve as a refreshing stop along a jogging route, offering fresh water year-round.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Atienza?
A: Atienza's diverse landscape is enjoyable throughout the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures for running, with vibrant natural colors. Summer mornings and evenings are also ideal to avoid the midday heat. Even in winter, the routes can be beautiful, though some higher elevation trails might require appropriate gear.

Q: Are there any routes that are part of a larger historical trail?
A: Yes, Atienza is part of &lt;strong&gt;El Camino del Cid (GR 160)&lt;/strong&gt;, a long-distance hiking and cycling route through medieval Spain. Segments of this historical path can be utilized for longer jogs, combining exercise with a journey through history.

Q: Can I find routes that take me through the Sierra de Pela?
A: Yes, Atienza's location in the Sierra de Pela provides access to trails within this mountain range. For example, the &lt;strong&gt;&lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1032365091/gr-167-pradena-de-atienza-to-cueva-del-oso-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;GR 167: Prádena de Atienza to Cueva del Oso Trail&lt;/a&gt;&lt;/strong&gt; leads through natural landscapes with varied elevation, offering a taste of the Sierra de Pela's beauty.
tips: Muslim settlement of the s. VIII to control neighboring Atienza, it consisted of a tower and a walled enclosure with a population on the slope of the hill, as shown by ceramics.
Fortress of Muslim origin located in the Spanish town of Atienza in the Castilian-La Mancha province of Guadalajara. It was built between the 11th and 12th centuries.
